plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs to zero.
Trim QQQI and QQQ to reduce overall index and income exposure.
Sell SMH to exit semiconductors entirely.
Trim CRWD to reduce tech concentration.
Buy AVGO as a strong, high-scoring tech name.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation by cutting riskier assets and building a cash buffer.
conservative.read: This plan aims for steady, low-drawdown growth, targeting around 1.5% per month. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, holding a larger cash buffer for safety.

plan: Trim SOXL to a minimal position, sell SPAL entirely.
Trim QQQI and QQQ to reduce income and broad index exposure.
Trim SMH to lower semiconductor concentration.
Buy AVGO and DDOG, both strong scanner setups.
This plan balances risk reduction with growth by trimming weaker areas and adding strong names.
It aims for risk-first growth with a moderate cash buffer.
balanced.read: This balanced approach targets around 2.5% monthly growth by reducing leverage and concentration while adding high-quality names. It maintains a moderate cash buffer for stability and aims for risk-first growth.

plan: Add to SOXL, and watch SPAL for potential addition, to increase leveraged exposure.
Sell QQQI to exit income, trim QQQ to reduce broad index.
Trim SMH to reduce overall semiconductor exposure slightly.
Buy AVGO and DDOG, both top-scoring scanner setups, to concentrate in strength.
This plan aggressively pursues growth by increasing leverage and concentrating in the best opportunities.
It maintains a small cash buffer and relies on strict stop discipline.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets around 4% monthly growth by leaning into leverage and concentrating in the highest-scoring opportunities, including leveraged ETFs. It carries significantly higher potential for both gains and losses, with a smaller cash buffer and the risk of rapid drawdowns due to leverage and ETF decay.

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to zero.
Add significantly to QQQI, focusing on income generation.
Trim QQQ and sell SMH and CRWD to exit growth-oriented tech.
Buy OXY, an accumulate-rated name, for income potential.
This plan prioritizes steady monthly income over growth.
It shifts the portfolio towards income-producing assets and reduces volatility.
income.read: This income-focused plan targets around 1% monthly in distributions by rotating into income ETFs and reducing high-beta growth assets. It prioritizes low drawdown and reliable income, though NAV can still fluctuate in market downturns.
